compiled_state.title: As a roadmap planning session, I can optionally pull Confluence context into the exploration phase by setting a configuration flag
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A config-gated dispatch of dso:confluence-researcher from roadmap SKILL.md, using the roadmap theme as the research seed.
**Why**: Roadmap sessions benefit from the same institutional Confluence knowledge as brainstorm sessions. Keeping this opt-in via a feature flag ensures no disruption to existing roadmap users who haven't configured Confluence.
**Scope**:
- IN: roadmap SKILL.md modification — at the start of the exploration phase, check confluence.roadmap_research_enabled in dso-config.conf; dispatch dso:confluence-researcher with the roadmap theme as context when flag=true; skip silently when flag is absent or false
- OUT: dso:confluence-researcher implementation (Story C owns that)
- OUT: brainstorm integration (Story C)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, if confluence.roadmap_research_enabled=true in dso-config.conf, roadmap SKILL.md dispatches dso:confluence-researcher at the start of the exploration phase and the Confluence research results are incorporated into the roadmap context
  ← Satisfies: "When confluence.roadmap_research_enabled=true in dso-config.conf, /dso:roadmap dispatches dso:confluence-researcher at the start of its exploration phase"
- When this story is complete, if confluence.roadmap_research_enabled is absent or false, roadmap SKILL.md proceeds without dispatching the researcher and no error is raised
  ← Satisfies: "When the flag is absent or false, the step is skipped silently"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] flag absent is the expected default state — the implementation must treat missing key as equivalent to false; do not require users to explicitly set false to disable
- [Compatibility] dso:confluence-researcher was designed primarily for brainstorm's "feature understanding" context; roadmap passes a "roadmap theme" — confirm the researcher's CQL keyword derivation produces relevant results when given roadmap-style input (broader thematic context vs. specific feature context); add a done definition confirming this if unclear after Story C is implemented
- [Integration] Verified: researcher is a stateless sub-agent; same agent can be dispatched from roadmap with different input context without any code changes to the researcher itself

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] Timeout and graceful degradation — Story C adds a curl --max-time 30 guard to the researcher, but the total agent dispatch time (CQL derivation + API call + summary generation) can exceed 30s. Story D must specify graceful degradation when the researcher times out or errors: roadmap SKILL.md should continue to the exploration phase without researcher results rather than failing the session. Add a done definition: 'When this story is complete, if dso:confluence-researcher times out or returns an error during roadmap dispatch, roadmap continues to its exploration phase with a logged warning and no researcher output.' (taxonomy: scope_overlaps)
comments.author: Claude
